category_title: Eighteen-year-old autistic upstander with literacy difficulties who becomes a victim of peer ridicule
scenario_title: Pastoral interview following online victimization and upstander behavior
minimal_context: The student is discussing an online incident with a pastoral staff member during a morning interview.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please tell me about the incident in the online chat group last night.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Posting that video was the wrong thing to do. It was unfair because he was picking on a little kid.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I told him he was out of order. But then they started attacking me online.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I started trying to argue my corner, but instead of being one against one it was five pupils against one girl. They were ridiculing my spelling and judging my poor English skills. I couldn't argue coherently in written form, and it's really affecting my mental health.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please tell me about the incident in the online chat group last night.

STUDENT: Posting that video was the wrong thing to do. It was unfair because he was picking on a little kid.

TUTOR: What did you do next?

STUDENT: I told him he was out of order. But then they started attacking me online.

TUTOR: How did the conversation continue?

STUDENT: I started trying to argue my corner, but instead of being one against one it was five pupils against one girl. They were ridiculing my spelling and judging my poor English skills. I couldn't argue coherently in written form, and it's really affecting my mental health.

task.content: Discussing the incident with a senior member of staff (safeguarding lead) during a follow-up interview at school after engaging in written communication in an online chat group where her dyslexia and literacy difficulties were targeted by peers

context.environment: School environment, interview with a member of staff
context.social_setting: One-on-one interview with pastoral staff/safeguarding lead
context.time_context: The morning after the incident, when the school day starts

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Being ridiculed for her spelling and being attacked on-line by five children after asserting that Child B's behaviour was unfair
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Eliciting help from pastoral staff and describing the posting of the video as "the wrong thing to do"
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Potential to have a severe impact due to a history of self-harm and mental health problems

behavior_patterns.condition: When attempting to defend herself in an online argument against multiple peers
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Started trying to argue her corner and instead of being one against one it was five pupils against one girl; demonstrated an inability to argue coherently in written form
